pattern: Simulate the reveal process in reverse on a sorted deck to reconstruct the required ordering using a queue of position indices.
hints: Sort the deck; the revealed cards must appear in increasing order, so the i-th smallest card occupies a specific position in the shuffled deck.
Simulate the queue of deck indices: repeatedly take the front index (place the next card there), then move the new front index to the back.
Fill the result array by assigning sorted values to the positions generated by the index queue simulation.

stress.description: random unique deck of given size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(1000, max(1, size))
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 1000001), n)
    return [vals]
